preamble: In the Pipil (Nawat) language of El Salvador, the signature Nahuan lateral affricate [tɬ] underwent a significant shift, often being realized as a labial fricative [f] or [ɸ] in certain environments, or eventually [h].

examples.note: Classical Nahuatl 'tl' often corresponds to 'f/h' in Nawat.

sources: Campbell, L. (1985). The Pipil Language of El Salvador.
Ward, J. S. (2012). The Pipil Language.
